ucs.title: [LLM] Lotus Blossom (Raspberry Typhoon) Sagerunex service persistence with cloud-service C2 (Dropbox/Twitter/Zimbra)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Sagerunex backdoor chain disclosed by Cisco Talos (2025) and Symantec (Bilbug, 2022): a DLL is registered as a Windows service via sc.exe / ServiceMain, then beacons through legitimate web APIs (api.dropboxapi.com, api.twitter.com, Zimbra webmail) instead of dedicated C2 — a tradecraft pattern that has remained stable across Elise/Emissary/Sagerunex variants used only by this cluster.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as svc_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=sc.exe OR Processes.process_name=powershell.exe) (Processes.process="*New-Service*" OR (Processes.process="*create*" AND Processes.process="*binPath*")) (Processes.process="*rundll32*" OR Processes.process="*svchost*" OR Processes.process="*.dll*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | rename dest as host | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as net_time values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_dest values(All_Traffic.app) as proc from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_host IN ("*dropboxapi.com","*content.dropbox.com","api.twitter.com","upload.twitter.com","*zimbra*")) (All_Traffic.app IN (rundll32.exe,svchost.exe,regsvr32.exe,wmiprvse.exe)) by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as host] | eval delta=net_time-svc_time | where delta>=0 AND delta<=86400 | table host user process parent_process_name svc_time net_time c2_dest proc delta
ucs.kql:
let cloudC2 = dynamic(["api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.twitter.com","upload.twitter.com"]);
let svcInstalls = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where (FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create" and ProcessCommandLine has "binPath")
   or (FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_cs "New-Service")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".dll","rundll32","svchost")
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, SvcTime=Timestamp, SvcCmd=ProcessCommandLine, AccountName;
let cloudBeacons = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (cloudC2) or RemoteUrl matches regex @"zimbra|/service/soap"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","svchost.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmiprvse.exe")
| project DeviceId, NetTime=Timestamp, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
svcInstalls
| join kind=inner cloudBeacons on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (SvcTime .. SvcTime + 1d)
| project DeviceName, AccountName, SvcTime, SvcCmd, NetTime, RemoteUrl, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by SvcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Generic 'service-installs-DLL' rules fire constantly. The actor-specific tell is the pairing of a freshly registered DLL service with outbound traffic to Dropbox/Twitter/Zimbra from rundll32/svchost — that exact combination is Sagerunex's documented C2 strategy and was used previously by Elise (Bilbug). Few legitimate workloads embed Twitter/Dropbox API calls inside svchost-hosted services, keepin

ucs.title: [LLM] Lotus Blossom (Bilbug) post-compromise toolkit: Venom proxy + Chrome cookie/credential theft followed by WinRAR staging
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's hands-on-keyboard sequence reported by Symantec/Cisco: deploying the open-source Venom proxy for internal pivoting (T1090.001), running a custom Chrome cookie stealer that touches Chrome's Login Data / Cookies SQLite (T1539), and finally staging archives with rar.exe / WinRAR before exfil (T1560.001, T1074.001). This three-step chain on the same host within hours is highly characteristic of Lotus Blossom intrusions in Hong Kong, Philippines, Vietnam and Taiwan government networks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as cookie_time values(Filesystem.process_name) as cookie_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Cookies*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*") Filesystem.process_name!=chrome.exe Filesystem.process_name!=msedge.exe by Filesystem.dest | rename Filesystem.* as * | rename dest as host | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as rar_time values(Processes.process) as rar_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN (rar.exe,winrar.exe,7z.exe) OR Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="* a -r *") by Processes.dest | rename Processes.* as * | rename dest as host] | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as proxy_time values(Processes.process) as proxy_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*venom*" OR (Processes.process="*-rhost*" AND Processes.process="*-rport*") OR Processes.process="*agent.exe*-lhost*") by Processes.dest | rename Processes.* as * | rename dest as host] | eval window=max(cookie_time,rar_time,proxy_time)-min(cookie_time,rar_time,proxy_time) | where window<=21600 | table host cookie_time cookie_proc proxy_time proxy_cmd rar_time rar_cmd window
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let cookieTouch = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\")
| where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","Login Data For Account")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","MsMpEng.exe")
| summarize CookieTime=min(Timestamp), CookieProc=any(InitiatingProcessFileName), CookieCmd=any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine) by DeviceId, DeviceName;
let venomLike = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("venom"," -lhost "," -lport "," -rhost "," -rport ") or FileName matches regex @"^(agent|admin)\.exe$"
| summarize ProxyTime=min(Timestamp), ProxyCmd=any(ProcessCommandLine) by DeviceId;
let rarStage = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" a ","-hp","-r ","-v","-m5")
| summarize RarTime=min(Timestamp), RarCmd=any(ProcessCommandLine) by DeviceId;
cookieTouch
| join kind=inner venomLike on DeviceId
| join kind=inner rarStage on DeviceId
| extend WindowSec = datetime_diff('second', max_of(CookieTime,ProxyTime,RarTime), min_of(CookieTime,ProxyTime,RarTime))
| where WindowSec <= 21600
| project DeviceName, CookieTime, CookieProc, CookieCmd, ProxyTime, ProxyCmd, RarTime, RarCmd, WindowSec
| order by CookieTime desc
ucs.rationale: Each leg in isolation is noisy (browsers touch their own SQLite all day, admins use 7z), but the conjunction — non-browser process opening Chrome Login Data + Venom-style proxy invocation + RAR with -hp on the same host inside 6 hours — is the documented Lotus Blossom / Bilbug intrusion fingerprint reported by Symantec (2022) against Asian governments and certificate authorities. Other China-nexus
